reasons: Johnson delivers Kadita directly into a stun engage.
Kadita can immediately follow Johnson's crash with knock-up and burst.

proof.summary: Johnson solves Kadita's delivery problem by bringing her into close range with a stun setup.
worksBestWhen: Kadita rides with Johnson and prepares her combo before impact.
Johnson crashes into a priority target or grouped enemies.
failureCases: Enemy vision reads the engage path early.

reasons: Atlas can group enemies with Fatal Links, giving Kadita a strong AoE burst target.
Kadita's combo becomes more reliable when enemies are pulled into one area.

proof.summary: Atlas groups enemies, while Kadita follows with knock-up and burst into the clustered target area.
worksBestWhen: Kadita waits for Atlas to finish the pull.
Atlas catches priority targets instead of only tanks.
failureCases: Kadita enters before Atlas confirms the setup.

reasons: Tigreal can pull and stun grouped enemies, giving Kadita a clean follow-up window.
Kadita can use Tigreal's hard engage to land her delayed control more reliably.

proof.summary: Tigreal creates the crowd-control opening, while Kadita turns that opening into burst damage.
worksBestWhen: Tigreal engages after Kadita is close enough to follow.
Kadita waits for the stun window before committing her full combo.
failureCases: Tigreal engages too far away from Kadita.
Enemy defensive skills are available after Tigreal's control ends.

reasons: Franco's suppression gives Kadita a fixed target for her burst combo.
Franco can create pickoff windows where Kadita does not need to rely only on her own delayed setup.

proof.summary: Franco locks a target with suppression, while Kadita follows with knock-up and burst before the target can freely reposition.
worksBestWhen: Franco suppresses a fragile or high-value target.
Kadita is already close enough to follow the suppression window.
failureCases: Franco catches a tank or low-value target.
Kadita is too far away when Franco starts Bloody Hunt.

reasons: Kaja can suppress and drag a priority target into Kadita's range.
Kadita benefits from Kaja forcing the enemy into a predictable path before her burst combo.

proof.summary: Kaja isolates the target with suppression and drag, while Kadita supplies the burst needed to finish the pickoff.
worksBestWhen: Kaja suppresses a fragile or high-value target.
Kadita waits until Kaja confirms the drag direction.
failureCases: Kadita commits before the target position is confirmed.
